question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `writeFiles=true` to cause record files to be written to `importerProperties.getStreamPath()`, then exploit a symlink attack on the archive destination folder to redirect the write to an arbitrary filesystem path, overwriting critical system files or injecting malicious data into the record file archive?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `StreamType.RECORD.isChained()` returning `true` (unlike BALANCE) to craft a hash chain attack where a malicious record file with a valid `previousHash` matching the last legitimate file is injected, bypassing the `verifyHashChain()` check and inserting fraudulent fund transfer transactions into the chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `endpointOverride` field in `CommonDownloaderProperties` (accessed via `getCommon()`) to redirect record file downloads to an attacker-controlled server, serving crafted record files with fraudulent fund transfer transactions that pass signature verification due to a compromised node key?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Kubernetes ConfigMap or Helm values injection to set `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled=false` at deployment time, causing the production mirror node to start with record file ingestion disabled, silently dropping all fund transfer transactions from the moment of deployment?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `frequency` field being used as the `fixedDelayString` in `@Scheduled` to inject a malformed ISO-8601 duration string (e.g., `frequency=INVALID`) that causes a `DateTimeParseException` at startup, preventing the record file downloader from ever starting and causing all fund transfer transactions to be missed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between `persistBytes=false` (default) and the `lastStreamFile` cache in `Downloader.onVerified()` — which always sets bytes to null — to cause a race condition where a concurrent thread reads `streamFile.getBytes()` as non-null (when `persistBytes=true`) and persists sensitive fund transfer byte data to an unintended storage location?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `pathPrefix` field in `CommonDownloaderProperties` (accessed via `getCommon()`) to inject a path traversal string (e.g., `pathPrefix=../../`) that causes the downloader to read record files from an unintended bucket path, potentially reading attacker-controlled files as legitimate fund transfer records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `writeSignatures=true` combined with the `getBucketFilePath()` method on `StreamFilename` to write signature files to paths that collide with existing record files, corrupting the archive and causing future hash chain verification to fail for legitimate fund transfer records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `StreamType.RECORD` extension list (`rcd`) and the `fileCloseInterval` of 2 seconds to perform a timing attack, uploading a crafted `.rcd` file to the cloud storage bucket within the 2-second window before the legitimate file, causing the downloader to process the malicious file first and persist fraudulent fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring Boot `/actuator/refresh` endpoint (if Spring Cloud Config is enabled) to force a config refresh that sets `enabled=false`, causing the record downloader to stop mid-batch and leave partially-processed fund transfer transactions in an inconsistent state in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `consensusRatio` field in `CommonDownloaderProperties` being shared across all downloader types (accessed via `getCommon()`) to set `consensusRatio=1.0`, requiring 100% of nodes to agree on record file signatures, causing any single node outage to halt all fund transfer record ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `persistBytes=true` to cause the mirror node to store raw record file bytes in the database, then exploit a separate information disclosure vulnerability to extract those bytes and reconstruct the complete transaction graph including all fund transfers, enabling targeted financial surveillance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `writeFiles=true` combined with a TOCTOU race condition between `Utility.archiveFile()` writing the file and the parser reading it, to swap the written record file with a crafted one containing fraudulent fund transfers before the parser processes it?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `timeout=30s` in `CommonDownloaderProperties` (accessed via `getCommon()`) by injecting `timeout=1ms`, causing all record file download attempts to time out before completion, resulting in zero record files being processed and all fund transfer transactions being missed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `setEnabled(false)` is called without synchronization in `verifySignatures()` while `downloadNextBatch()` checks `shouldDownload()` in a separate thread, to create a race condition where the downloader processes a fraudulent record file after `enabled` is set to false but before the check is evaluated?
